name: wilson-hexa-first-warn
description: PreToolUse guard on Write AND Bash when a new file in the protected set is about to land — acts on the hexa-first principle, pointing the agent at `.hexa` as the primary replacement (`.hexa` absorbs scripting + firmware authoring in this project), or at hexa-lang upstream PR-only (`hexa atlas register <file>` → `hexa atlas pr`) when no existing path fits. Protected extensions: scripting (`.py`, `.sh`) plus firmware (C: `.c` `.h` `.cpp` `.hpp` `.cc` `.hh`; assembly: `.s` `.S`; RTL: `.v` `.sv` `.vhd` `.vhdl`). General-app languages (.rs, .go, .java, .js, .ts, …) stay out of scope to avoid noise. Two tool surfaces — both routed to the same decision logic: `Write` (tool_input.file_path matches a protected ext) and `Bash` (tool_input.command contains a `>` / `>>` redirect or `tee [-a]` whose target is a path ending in a protected ext — closes the heredoc/redirect bypass like `cat > foo.py <<EOF`). Two modes, selected by SIDECAR_HEXA_FIRST_WARN_MODE (WILSON_ accepted): `block` (DEFAULT) — the call is DENIED at PreToolUse via permissionDecision, emitting an AI-native single-line directive (HEXA_FIRST_BLOCK { trigger, target, ext, primary, extend, downgrade }); the model must write `.hexa` instead, extend upstream via PR, or explicitly downgrade. `warn` — a single-line advisory (HEXA_FIRST_WARN { ... }) is injected via additionalContext and the call proceeds unchanged. Any value other than `warn` resolves to `block` — block is the floor. New-file moment only (`Write` matcher, NOT `Edit`) — editing an existing protected file does not nag every time. Known residual bypass (not covered yet): mv/cp/ln rename of a tmp file into a protected ext path. Implements hexa-first as enforcement complementing wilson-prefs's principle-text injection — text says "prefer hexa", this stops at the file-creation moment. Opt out entirely: SIDECAR_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1 (or WILSON_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1).
